name: Project.Feature.UI.LogWriteNotificationDisplay_Feature
observations: Displays file write notifications in Telnet tab showing content written to .lis/.fbc/.log/.rpc files.

name: Project.Method.UI.CommanderWindow_OnLogWriteNotification
observations: Handler method that receives log write completion signals and displays formatted notifications in Telnet tab.
Format: '📝 Written to {filename}: {lines_written} new line(s) | Total: {total_lines} lines' for success with content, '❌ Failed to write to {filename}' for failures.

name: Project.Pattern.UI.SignalBasedNotificationDisplay_Pattern
observations: Pattern: Connect service-level signal (log_write_completed) to UI handler that formats and displays notifications without modifying service or display components.
Benefits: Loose coupling, reusable display method, centralized formatting logic in window controller following MVC pattern.

Removed SessionType.VNC enum value and session_types registry entry.

name: Project.Pattern.Refactoring.ComponentRemovalPattern_Pattern
observations: Systematic component removal: delete files first -> remove imports -> clean references bottom-up (UI->presenter->service).
Prevents dangling references by following dependency order. Validates with syntax checks + tests.

name: Project.Feature.NodeTree.StartupColorPersistence
observations: Implemented persistent file coloring that survives application restarts by checking existing file content when tree loads.
Integration: _check_file_color_on_startup method called from _create_file_item, uses LogWriter.get_file_line_count to read content.
Color thresholds: 0 lines=red (no content), <10 lines=yellow (minimal), â‰¥10 lines=green (sufficient content).

name: Project.Method.NodeTree.CheckFileColorOnStartup
observations: Method in NodeTreePresenter that applies colors to file items based on existing content during tree population.
Located in src/commander/presenters/node_tree_presenter.py, called after file_item_map insertion.
Logic: Check file existence â†’ count lines â†’ apply color (red/yellow/green) â†’ log action.

name: Project.Pattern.NodeTree.DualModeColoring
observations: Pattern implementing two coloring modes: startup mode (content-only) and runtime mode (execution-aware).
Startup mode validates existing files without command status, runtime mode uses command_success + log_success + line_count.
Both modes converge on same visual result for files with content, ensuring color consistency across restarts.

name: Project.Commander.NodeTree.Feature_HierarchicalIconColoring
observations: Dual color system for commander node tree: (1) Icon color based on command execution status (2) Text color based on file content lines. Icons use rectangles for files/sections, circles for nodes. Hierarchical aggregation propagates icon colors upward (file→section→node) using red-priority logic.

Architecture: Separated update_node_color (text/foreground) from update_node_icon (QIcon rectangles/circles). Icon colors stored in UserRole['icon_color'] for aggregation. Three aggregation methods: _aggregate_hierarchical_colors (trigger), _aggregate_section_color (files→section), _aggregate_node_color (sections→node).

Implementation: Added 9 icon generator functions in icons.py (get_file_icon_*/get_section_icon_*/get_node_icon_* for green/yellow/red). Modified _check_and_update_node_color to update both icon (execution) and text (content check via get_file_line_count). Integration points: command completion and tree loading.

name: Project.Commander.NodeTree.Method_update_node_icon
observations: View method in NodeTreeView that updates rectangle/circle icon color based on command execution status. Accepts item and color_name, auto-detects item type from UserRole data. Uses get_node_icon_* for type='node' (circles), get_file_icon_* for files/sections (rectangles).
Independent from update_node_color (text color). Called by presenter after command execution. Supports green/yellow/red colors. Uses setIcon(0, icon) to change QIcon decoration on QTreeWidgetItem.

Integration: Called from _check_and_update_node_color in presenter after determining icon_color based on command success and line counts. Icon color stored in UserRole['icon_color'] for hierarchical aggregation consumption.

name: Project.Commander.NodeTree.Pattern_DualColorSystem
observations: Separation of concerns pattern: Icon color (execution status, aggregates hierarchically) vs Text color (file content, independent). Both updated after command completion but serve different purposes and use different update methods.

Icon aggregation logic: Red priority (any child red → parent red), All-green rule (all children green → parent green), Yellow fallback (mixed states). Aggregation traverses from file items through section items to node items using parent() relationships.

Data flow: Command completes → _check_and_update_node_color determines icon_color (execution) and text_color (content) → stores icon_color in UserRole → triggers _aggregate_hierarchical_colors → sections/nodes aggregate child icon colors → visual hierarchy reflects execution state.

name: Project.Commander.WorkflowControl.Feature_PrintAllNodesPauseResumeCancel
observations: Implemented pause/resume/cancel controls for Print All Nodes workflow in NodeTreePresenter.
State flags (_workflow_paused, _workflow_cancelled) checked between node processing cycles.
Pause/Resume/Cancel affect workflow between nodes; current node completes before state change.

name: Project.Commander.WorkflowControl.Method_HandlePause
observations: Sets _workflow_paused flag and notifies SequentialCommandProcessor for dual-path support.
Emits user-friendly message: 'Workflow paused - will pause after current node completes'.
Located in NodeTreePresenter class at line ~1506.

name: Project.Commander.WorkflowControl.Method_HandleResume
observations: Resumes Print All Nodes workflow from paused state without skipping nodes.
Located in NodeTreePresenter class at line ~1517.

name: Project.Commander.WorkflowControl.Method_HandleCancel
observations: Sets _workflow_cancelled flag, clears _nodes_to_process list to stop workflow.
Reports count of skipped nodes in status message for user feedback.
Located in NodeTreePresenter class at line ~1530.

name: Project.Commander.WorkflowControl.Pattern_StateFlagBetweenCycles
observations: Pattern: Check workflow state flags between processing cycles, not during execution.
Allows current operation to complete gracefully before honoring pause/cancel request.
Avoids complex thread interruption and resource cleanup issues with QThreadPool workers.

name: Project.Commander.UI.Feature_ButtonStateManagement
observations: Button state management for Print All Nodes workflow - enables pause/resume/cancel buttons at workflow lifecycle points since ExecutionState signals not emitted by command_queue path.

Architecture: Manual button enable/disable calls in NodeTreePresenter (process_all_nodes_print_commands, _process_next_node_in_sequence, _handle_pause, _handle_resume, _handle_cancel) control button states throughout workflow.

Integration: Direct button property access via self.view.pause_btn.setEnabled(), self.view.resume_btn.setEnabled(), self.view.cancel_btn.setEnabled() bypasses update_control_buttons() method which requires ExecutionState signals.

name: Project.Commander.UI.Method_ManualButtonStateControl
observations: Manual button enable/disable implementation in 6 locations: process_all_nodes_print_commands (start+error), _process_next_node_in_sequence (cancel+complete), _handle_pause, _handle_resume, _handle_cancel.
Pattern: self.view.pause_btn.setEnabled(True/False), self.view.resume_btn.setEnabled(True/False), self.view.cancel_btn.setEnabled(True/False) with debug logging for troubleshooting.
State transitions: IDLE (all disabled) -> RUNNING (pause+cancel enabled) -> PAUSED (resume+cancel enabled) -> RUNNING (pause+cancel enabled) -> IDLE (all disabled).

name: Project.Commander.UI.Pattern_ButtonLifecycleStates
observations: Button state lifecycle pattern for workflow control: IDLE (❌❌❌), RUNNING (✅❌✅), PAUSED (❌✅✅), CANCELLED/COMPLETED (❌❌❌) maps button availability to workflow execution state.

Root cause: update_control_buttons() method exists but only responds to sequential_processor.execution_state_changed signal - Print All Nodes uses command_queue directly without ExecutionState emission.
Solution pattern: Directly control button states at workflow lifecycle hooks rather than relying on signal-based architecture when ExecutionState signals unavailable.

name: Global.Architecture.Pattern_DualExecutionPaths
observations: Dual execution architecture: Path A (Print All Nodes -> command_queue directly) vs Path B (context menu -> sequential_processor with ExecutionState signals) requires different button state management strategies.
Path A limitation: No ExecutionState signal emission means update_control_buttons() never called, requiring manual button state control at workflow lifecycle points.
Design insight: When bypassing abstraction layers (sequential_processor), must manually replicate side effects (button state updates) that abstraction provides automatically.

name: Project.Commander.Debugging.Method_ButtonStateDiagnostics
observations: Debug logging added for button state transitions: 'Print All Nodes: Enabled/Disabled buttons for STATE' messages enable troubleshooting of button enable/disable execution flow.
Log patterns: 'Enabled pause and cancel buttons' (start), 'Updated buttons for PAUSED/RUNNING/CANCELLED state' (transitions), 'Disabled all buttons' (completion/cancellation).
Diagnostic strategy: Search debug.log for 'Print All Nodes:' prefix to trace button state lifecycle during workflow execution and identify missing state transitions.

name: Project.Workflow.Optimization.Feature_ChatmodeOptimizationWorkflow
observations: Workflow for optimizing chatmode instructions via batch workflow log analysis and compliance measurement.
User specifies target chatmode file, workflow analyzes all logs/workflow_*.md files, generates optimization recommendations.
6-phase architecture: Discovery(0) → Completion Analysis(1) → Chatmode Evaluation(2) → Optimization Planning(3) → Instruction Updates(4) → Validation(5).

name: Project.Workflow.Optimization.Method_WorkflowComplianceScoring
observations: Scoring system for completion format compliance: present+complete=1.0, present+partial=0.5, missing=0.0.
Identifies instruction gaps: missing field pattern (≥40%), partial field pattern (≥30%), quality issues (low-value content).

name: Project.Workflow.Optimization.Pattern_GenericChatmodeOptimization
observations: User-defined chatmode target (not hardcoded) enables workflow reuse across multiple chatmode files.
Batch workflow log analysis from logs/workflow_*.md provides aggregate compliance statistics and trend analysis.
Four-tier prioritization: Critical (mandatory <60%), High (quality <70%), Medium (consistency), Low (enhancements).
Optimization report template with before/after metrics, specific recommendations, predicted improvements.
